step_by_step_thinking: Disagree; while it’s true that hepcidin regulates iron levels mainly via interaction with ferroportin, this regulation also indirectly affects divalent metal transporter 1 (DMT1), which plays an important role in duodenal enterocytes where iron uptake occurs.
raw_response:
Disagree; while it’s true that hepcidin regulates iron levels mainly via interaction with ferroportin, this regulation also indirectly affects divalent metal transporter 1 (DMT1), which plays an important role in duodenal enterocytes where iron uptake occurs. Therefore, considering all roles together, both direct and indirect effects need to be considered. My updated answer remains D based on current understanding but acknowledges broader context. Answer Choice: D.
